methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Occam is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Ana Montero Horas and Andre Marques-Smith, having raised $14M to date. The target operates within a competitive Biotech & Health sector represented by 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Of three valuation methods tested, only stage-progression triangulation proved applicable. This method uses the $14M total raised as a value proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression in biotech, implying a $35M post-money valuation. Peer-median analysis was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum sample thresholds—while 16 same-sector peers exist, filtering for comparable stage and tier yielded fewer than the required 3 datapoints. Public-comp anchoring was similarly unavailable, as Occam's revenue is not on file; the sector median P/S of 7.8x across public tickers cannot be meaningfully applied without this denominator.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $30M to $40M, with a median of $35M. This range reflects a single methodological input and should be treated as indicative rather than definitive. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer metrics at seed stage, lack of revenue disclosure, and reliance on a single multiplier assumption. Additional diligence on product-market fit and burn rate would strengthen conviction.
